extra.reason: NodeStateSummary published per tailer, SSE state events on /trace, GET /state + /state/node with DumpNode round-trip, subquery emitted counters; 65 tests pass, zero new clippy warnings
Unified truthful graph (engine node ids 1:1), SSE-fed state chips via state-store, deep dumps in DetailPanel, deleted build-dbsp/useAggValues; tsc + vite build clean
Verified on private stack: chips update live on insert/delete (agg 29→30→29, multiset 49→48), family/agg dumps render, ≤3 connections

pipeline-viz README rewritten (single view, state model, /state endpoints, removed stale live-toggle), root README + ARCHITECTURE + AGENTS refreshed, in-app explainers rewritten in node-meta.ts/DetailPanel
Rebuild complete: engine state surface + reactive one-view visualizer + docs; verified E2E with Playwright

Circuit view re-added, engine-authoritative: /graph emits operators+opEdges with hop+state bindings; viz renders via build-circuit.ts with engine-declared trace/fresh expansion; op explainers in panel; 10s state safety re-seed added (lossy trace channel); verified live in browser (chips + FlashNode render confirmed); 66 engine tests pass

dbsp/rkyv/feldera-size-of/feldera-macros removed; Tup2/ZWeight defined locally in value.rs; vestigial derives dropped; ordered-float de-pinned; stale circuit comments and docs realigned. cargo build + 124 tests pass; dbsp gone from Cargo.lock.

Added pg::Pool (semaphore-capped per-URL pool, idle reuse, ROLLBACK-on-checkin). Backfills, subquery query-backs, and subset queries now share it; ELECTRIC_DB_POOL_SIZE wired (default 20, was a no-op). Engine tests + full conformance (141) pass.

Standalone shapes now indexed by necessary conjunct (AccessLeaf: Eq hash buckets, range bounds in BTreeMaps, fallback scan for OR/NOT/LIKE/neq). process_envelope visits only candidates on the untraced hot path; full scan kept under trace so visualizer hops are unchanged. Unit tests + full conformance green.

on_table_delta now returns flip work; a single engine flip-propagator task runs query-backs with the registry lock NEVER held across PG round-trips (eval+append stay atomic under it — required for convergence). pending_flips counter exposed via /replication/lsn; conformance drainEngine gained stage 3. Lock sharding deliberately skipped: post-change hold spans are in-memory + appends only, and sharding a cross-shard DAG adds complexity with little gain at this scale. 141/141 conformance green.

replication.rs rewritten: streams a pgoutput slot via pgwire-replication (walsender push, SCRAM, keepalive/feedback) + in-repo pgoutput.rs decoder (proto v1, text-mode tuples deliberately — preserves byte-identity with ::text backfill; binary would force re-stringifying). append-then-acknowledge replaces peek-then-advance; ensure_slot migrates test_decoding slots; publication <slot>_pub created at setup. All decoder+envelope unit tests added; 141/141 conformance green incl. live replication + fuzz. Docs realigned.

Ingestor appends whole commits in order to the single 'changes' stream; ONE LSN-ordered sequencer task replaces per-table tailers (per-table exec state, global (lsn,seq) dedupe, per-transaction atomic flush before the next commit — cross-table). Shape creation is two-phase (pending buffer -> concurrent pooled backfill -> gated activation) so backfills never stall the pipeline. Library-mode ingest + harness barrier moved to the change log. 141/141 conformance + Electric oracle test green. Docs realigned.

Durable shape catalog (meta/catalog stream: created/joined/left/dropped + offset checkpoints via ordered writer task) replayed at boot; plain/routed shapes resume via change-log replay + passthrough gates, aggregates re-seed fresh; subquery shapes dropped loudly (follow-up bead filed for inner-node persistence). Disk-budget undercount fixed earlier (st_blocks du + compose ELECTRIC_STORAGE_DIR wiring). New conformance-restart test proves crash-restore end-to-end; full suite 142/142 green.

All verification done. Electric conformance: oracle 0F, property 0F, subqueries 15/2F (= documented row-tags baseline). Fleet: all 5 benchmarks pass, every metric improved vs 2026-07-02 baseline (creation p99 5022->2806ms scale-1; the report's 10.7s scale-2 p99 now 5.6s); external-electric mode + AGENTS.md instructions added. Browser MCP session: LinearLite live end-to-end (create issue, psql write -> fold 601->602 live), viz logical+circuit views + DumpNode deep dumps work; subquery leave/join verified against PG ground truth (engine exactly right: 204 move-outs, /query + /v1/shape = 400); found+fixed a client-side stale-aggregate-callback race (badge freeze; guarded in packages/client + useShape, repro now converges 603 stable). Final gates: 9 cargo suites ok + 142/142 vitest.
duplicate of dbsp-ds-09u

Implemented and verified: dynamic (membership-subquery) shapes + COUNT aggregates served from the circuit; conformance 101/101 in all three modes; live demo verified incl. move-in/out (23-row group) and counts tick 22→23. Static/match-all deliberately stay on the indexed legacy tiers (see commit 5c1189b).

Episode 03-serving-model delivered: three tiers, todo-model walkthrough, static pipeline graph explorer walk with dynamic shape connections; tutorials index links to it. Acceptance criteria met (walk, not embedded screenshot, matching the series' live-canvas style).

engine.rs (6107 lines) split into engine/ module dir: mod.rs 837, sequencer.rs 1120, lifecycle.rs 978, introspection.rs 903, executors.rs 410, circuit_serving.rs 342, catalog.rs 349, planning.rs 204, output.rs 101, tests.rs 929. Pure code movement + mechanical pub(crate) widening; zero sibling-file changes (crate::engine::* paths preserved via re-exports). Gates: cargo test 138 lib + all integration green; full vitest 161/161 incl. conformance. Normalized-content diff proves zero lines lost. Docs realigned (file maps, prose refs, AGENTS.md) in same pass.

Shared membership kernel extracted (engine/membership.rs): fold_refcount_flips (flip detection, shared Flip/FlipDir vocabulary with the registry), query_rows_by_col/query_rows_all (arrangement-snapshot -> pooled-Postgres fallback, now used by BOTH circuit cohort serving and the subquery registry), latest_rows_by_pk (absolute-emission front half). Fixed a real bug in passing: circuit path previously log-and-skipped on a missed arrangement move lookup (silent move-miss / permanent shape divergence); it now falls back to Postgres. New regression test pins both implementations to identical flips for the same change sequence (membership_flips_agree_between_refcount_and_contributor_set). Gates: cargo 142 lib + integration green; vitest 161/161 incl. conformance; Electric oracle 0 failures; live e2e on the demo verified BOTH tiers (circuit-served IN: 307->409->307; registry-served NOT IN: 205->103->205; pendingFlips drained to 0). Docs file maps updated.

Aggregate tier unified + indexed. (1) One shared wire-envelope builder (engine/output.rs::agg_envelope) now emits for BOTH tiers — AggShape (in-engine fold) and CircuitAgg (counts pipeline) delegate to it, removing the copy-pasted format that had only a comment keeping it in sync; regression test agg_envelope_shared_wire_format pins the contract. (2) Aggregates are conjunct-indexed like the standalone tier: TableExec.agg_index (a second StandaloneIndex) selects candidate aggregates per change, so aggregate count is no longer O(N) per change; match-all/un-indexable predicates stay on the scan list; index bypassed under trace so every node still reports hops; insert/remove maintained at activation and RemoveShape. Test agg_index_candidates_prune covers pruning+removal. Gates: cargo all green (incl. 2 new tests), vitest 161/161 conformance, Electric oracle 0 failures, live demo e2e: indexed COUNT(status=done) + match-all COUNT(*) tracked insert/update/delete exactly (non-matching insert did not touch the indexed agg; final counts returned to baseline). Cost-model doc updated (internals §3.6).

Decision written to docs/ivm-engine-internals.md §6b: NO interpreted operator graph for the dynamic tier. dbsp is already the composition engine (new template kinds go to arrangements.rs); flatness is the design (routing is an index lookup, not dataflow — a graph reintroduces the per-node linear scan the design avoids); executor kinds are stable so there is no variation to abstract; the composability mechanism is the shared-kernel pattern (membership.rs, agg_envelope) with cross-path regression tests. Revisit iff runtime-defined per-shape pipelines enter the roadmap. Optional follow-up noted: derive visualizer OpNodes from executors via a trait.

All four children done: .1 engine.rs split into engine/ module tree (zero behavior change, external surface preserved); .2 shared membership kernel (+ fixed silent move-miss); .3 aggregate tier unified on one wire envelope + conjunct-indexed (no longer O(N)/change); .4 operator-graph decision recorded (no — flat tier + kernels by design). Every phase gated on cargo + full vitest conformance + Electric oracle + live demo e2e.
Closed

Fixed in https://github.com/balegas/electric-ivm/pull/26. Root cause was not unstable loadMore/hasMore identities (already memoized) but the register state-mirror itself: the subset tail applies rows one-by-one, and the feed components' effect copied each new rows identity into BrowseList state (setFeeds) — an unbroken passive-effect→setState chain ~200 links long that trips React's nested-update cap. Fix: BrowseList consumes useSubset directly (one active feed at a time; hook re-keys on the def), register/FeedState indirection deleted; plus a one-in-flight guard on useSubset.loadMore (the per-row onEndReached storm batched ~41 duplicate page fetches into one URL → HTTP 431). Verified live via Playwright: warning reproduced on main with a single Leave click; after the fix, user-switch + all-projects Join/Leave churn (through zero membership), project view, board view, and 200→400→600 scroll paging all run with zero console errors.

Row data moved back to Postgres. arrangements.rs is now a counts-only in-memory dbsp circuit (O(distinct groups)), reseeded each boot from one group-aggregated REPEATABLE READ snapshot per table (pg::backfill_group_counts, O(groups) not O(rows), SnapshotGate-fenced). Row arrangements + storage/spill/checkpoints/meta.json/layout-fingerprint deleted; INDEXES + storage knobs deprecated (warn-and-ignore). Cohort membership tier removed — all membership subqueries registry-served (one implementation); row lookups = pooled PG via the membership kernel. Engine holds nothing row-scale. Gates: cargo/vitest-conformance/Electric-oracle green; demo e2e exact; fleet bench 4/5 better than baseline (creation p50 766->398, latency 64->25), subquery creation storm slower by the inherent PG-seed trade + upstream DS create serialization (durable-streams#394). PR branch feat/rows-in-postgres-parallel-querybacks.

Query-backs parallelized with ordering preserved. Flip propagation: semaphore-bounded worker pool (ELECTRIC_IVM_FLIP_WORKERS, default 8), PG round-trips concurrent (bounded by ELECTRIC_DB_POOL_SIZE), never under the registry lock. Emission: per-stream FIFO lanes (engine/emission.rs) — envelopes enqueued under the registry lock so per-shape append order = eval order (the old atomicity guarantee, no network under the lock); pendingFlips barrier covers queued batches (test: emission_lanes_order_and_barrier). Subquery-shape creation three-phase: PG seeding/backfill outside the lock, delta buffering + gated replay at install, conflict-retry for shared mid-seed nodes; ensure_stream out of the state lock. Data placement invariants held through all gates incl. batched-mutation + concurrency conformance.

Re-tested per goal: NOT reproducible with in-memory Rust DS — 300 concurrent creates = 0.13s wall / p50 17ms (--durability memory, Linux container) vs 1.15s / 225ms in the original macOS wal repro. Linux wal is also fast (0.20s / 22ms), so the ~4ms/create serialization is dominated by macOS fsync (F_FULLFSYNC) on the create path, not an architectural no-group-commit defect (Docker-VM fsync caveat noted). Findings posted upstream: electric-sql/electric#4703 (left open for the maintainer — group-committing creates would still fix the macOS/slow-disk case). Repo-side exposure is minimal since #29 made in-memory the default; residual: macOS dev machines running wal during creation storms (the fleet bench environment).
Fixed, verified on hosted infra, report corrected and republished

Implemented and verified on branch feat/subquery-circuit-templates (6 commits, not pushed). Membership sets + flip detection moved into a dedicated dbsp membership circuit; nodes grouped by parameterized templates (equality literals lifted as binds). Gates: cargo suites green (147 lib + integration), vitest oracle conformance 162/162, Electric oracle suite pass, Electric subqueries suite 13/15 = pre-existing baseline (2 known row-tags protocol gaps, documented in electric-conformance/README). LinearLite driven via Playwright: template sharing across users, live move-in/move-out on join/leave, issue creation, visualizer + node dumps clean.

Phase 0 complete: heap_size accounting (bytes on-demand after sampler fix 66117f2), attribution runbook (verdict: dbsp circuit residency dominates ~40%), G2 gate tests, iteration log with 0/0b rows. Gate decision: Phase 1 starts at Task 1.3; feed keys 27% keep Phase 2.

Resolved by iteration-1 A/B at 100k subs: FEED_TRACE on-vs-off delta is ~10-13 MiB (~1%), the historical 323 MiB was an era artifact (creation-peak ps rss, pre-audit code). Shared-integral mechanism confirmed; S3 updated, off-for-memory recommendation withdrawn.

Phase 1 closed at gate after Task 1.3 only: no pinning bug, 8x is representation cost (3x Value/Row layout x 2.1x dbsp double-buffering), integral/snapshots observability split shipped. Gate decision: skip 1.1 (allocator, 11-16% slack, deferred until after Phase 2 changes the base) and 1.2 (interning, owned metadata 8.8-15.6% < 25%); proceed to Phase 2 pk dictionary (118 B/entry measured, feed keys ~27%).

Superseded by measurements: Task 1.3 disproved the double-copy premise (feed trace shares the operator integral via TraceId cache; FEED_TRACE A/B delta ~1%), and Task 2.1 (pk dictionary, u32 keys) took the feed entry to 24 B / ~9 B owned per synced row — beyond parity with the old known_members HashSets (~85 B/row). Remaining 100k-scale footprint is dbsp per-entry residency overhead, tracked by the phase-2 gate work.

Phase 2 complete: PkDict (123->24 B/entry), FeedSet Roaring host-side (2.2 B/entry owned, feed relation out of the circuit), storage-cache default 512->64 MiB. Cumulative @100k subs: footprint 1053->632 MiB steady (-40%), 6.6 KiB/subscription, owned synced-row 2.2 B (-97%). Plan closed; Phases 3/4 obsoleted (target term now 6.4 MiB).

Moot: the feed relation left the dbsp circuit (Task 2.2 FeedSet); bytes_feed_sets reports exact owned bytes and FEED_TRACE no longer exists. Remaining circuit bytes are exact via dbsp sizing (Task 1.3 split).
